# **AWS RDS MySQL Integration with Java Web Application**

## **Project Overview**

This project demonstrates the deployment of a **Java-based Login and User Registration web application** on **Amazon EC2**, integrated with **Amazon RDS MySQL** as the backend database.

The application code was deployed from the GitHub repository:

https://github.com/CharantejAkri/aws-rds-java.git

The main objective of this project is to validate end-to-end connectivity between:

* **EC2 (Application Server / MySQL Client)**  
* **Amazon RDS MySQL (Database Server)**  
* **Java JSP-based Login & Registration Application**

The project also verifies whether the user registration details are successfully stored in the database.

Amazon RDS provides a managed MySQL database instance that can be securely accessed from an application server running on EC2.

---

# **Architecture**

User Browser  
     │  
     ▼  
EC2 Instance (Tomcat \+ Java App)  
     │  
     ▼  
Amazon RDS MySQL  
Database: jwt  
Table: users

---

# **AWS Services Used**

* **Amazon EC2**  
  * Hosts the Java web application  
  * Acts as the application server and MySQL client  
* **Amazon RDS (MySQL)**  
  * Managed relational database service  
  * Stores login and registration details  
* **Security Groups**  
  * Allows EC2 → RDS communication on port **3306**  
  * Allows browser access on port **8080**  
* **VPC**  
  * Enables secure network communication between EC2 and RDS

---

# **Deployment Steps**

## **1\. Launch EC2 Instance**

An Ubuntu EC2 instance was launched to host the application.

### **Installed Components**

sudo apt update  
sudo apt install openjdk-11-jdk \-y  
sudo apt install tomcat9 \-y  
sudo apt install mysql-client-core-8.0 \-y  
![][image1]

---

## **2\. Create Amazon RDS MySQL Instance**

A MySQL RDS instance was created with the following configuration:

| Parameter | Value |
| ----- | ----- |
| Engine | MySQL |
| Port | 3306 |
| Database Name | jwt |
| Username | admin |

![][image2]  
![][image3]  
![][image4]  
![][image5]  
![][image6]

---

## **3\. Configure Security Groups**

### **EC2 Security Group**

| Type | Port |
| ----- | ----- |
| SSH | 22 |
| HTTP | 8080 |

### **RDS Security Group**

| Type | Port | Source |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| MySQL/Aurora | 3306 | EC2 Security Group |

This configuration allows the application server to communicate securely with the RDS database.

![][image7]

---

## **4\. Deploy Application from GitHub**

The project code was cloned from the repository:

git clone [https://github.com/CharantejAkri/aws-rds-java.git](https://github.com/CharantejAkri/aws-rds-java.git)

Update login.jsp and userregistration.jsp in the src/main/webapp/

![][image8]  
![][image9]

The application files were copied into Tomcat webapps:

sudo cp \-r aws-rds-java/LoginWebApp /var/lib/tomcat9/webapps/

Restart Tomcat:

sudo systemctl restart tomcat9

---

# **Database Table Creation**

The `users` table was created manually inside the `jwt` database.

USE jwt;

CREATE TABLE users (  
    id INT AUTO\_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,  
    first\_name VARCHAR(100),  
    last\_name VARCHAR(100),  
    email VARCHAR(150),  
    username VARCHAR(100),  
    password VARCHAR(100),  
    regdate DATE  
);

Verification:

SHOW TABLES;

Output:

Users  
![][image10]

---

# **Application URLs**

## **Registration Page**

http://\<EC2-Public-IP\>:8080/LoginWebApp/userRegistration.jsp

## **Login Page**

http://\<EC2-Public-IP\>:8080/LoginWebApp/login.jsp  
![][image11]

![][image12]

---

# **Database Verification**

After submitting the registration form, the data was verified in Amazon RDS.

Login to MySQL client from EC2:

mysql \-h \<rds-endpoint\> \-u admin \-p  
![][image13]

Run:

USE jwt;  
SELECT \* FROM users;

Example output:

\+----+------------+-----------+-------------------+----------+----------+------------+  
| id | first\_name | last\_name | email             | username | password | regdate    |  
\+----+------------+-----------+-------------------+----------+----------+------------+  
|  1 | Charan     | Byna      | charan@email.com  | charan   | \*\*\*\*\*\*   | 2026-04-16 |  
\+----+------------+-----------+-------------------+----------+----------+------------+

This confirms that the user registration details are successfully stored in the database.

![][image14]

![][image15]

---

# **Testing Outcome**

## **Registration Test**

* User form submitted successfully  
* Data inserted into `users` table  
* Redirected to welcome page

## **Login Test**

* User credentials validated successfully  
* Login page functioning as expected

![][image16]

![][image17]

---

# **Key Learnings**

* Launching and configuring EC2  
* Creating and configuring RDS MySQL  
* Security group communication  
* Deploying Java web application on Tomcat  
* Connecting application server with RDS  
* Verifying data persistence in database

---

# **Conclusion**

This project successfully demonstrates deployment of a **Java Login and User Registration web application** using:

* **Amazon EC2 as application server**  
* **Amazon RDS MySQL as backend database**  
* **Tomcat as web server**

The application was successfully tested by verifying that user registration data is stored in the RDS database.
